summary:
• Iran nuclear deal uncertainty and escalating geopolitical tensions continue to create oil price volatility that directly impacts Western Hemisphere export competitiveness and downstream margins.

• Critical chokepoint disruptions—particularly around the Strait of Hormuz—are forcing global energy flows to reroute through alternative supply corridors and driving unprecedented demand for U.S. fuel exports and non-traditional energy sources.

• Regulatory policy shifts, including suspended methane rules and climate litigation pressures on major oil producers, are reshaping capital allocation and investment priorities across Americas upstream and downstream operations.
